narration: Transform a loop-based algorithm to use recursion instead of a loop.
Cache the results of the recursive algorithm using memorization to simplify time complexity.
Use two examples: runtime and factorial algorithms.
Recursion is a function calling itself, useful for repeated tasks like loops.
Recursion is preferable for traversing tree data structures due to its structure.
Recursion creates a tree-like structure of function calls.
All recursive functions require a base case to prevent stack overflow.
Without a base case, the call stack overflows, causing a stack overflow error.
Implement a base case by incrementing a counter before the function calls itself.
The base case is when the counter is greater than four, limiting function calls to five.
If a function returns a value, the stack unwinds from top to bottom, negating the return value.
Make each function return the recursive function it invokes to propagate the return value up the stack.
Memorization caches a function's return value based on its input parameters.
Caching prevents redundant calculations, improving time complexity.
Use a JavaScript closure to store cache data privately instead of global scope.
Initialize the closure on document load to ensure proper behavior.
Implement the iterative Fibonacci algorithm using a loop.
The iterative method calculates each number as the sum of the previous two.
Implement the recursive Fibonacci algorithm with a base case for n < 2.
The recursive algorithm creates a tree-like structure of function calls.
The recursive Fibonacci algorithm has exponential time complexity (2^n).
Implement the memoized recursive Fibonacci algorithm using a closure.
The memoized algorithm stores calculated values in a cache object to avoid redundant calculations.
Implement the recursive factorial algorithm with a base case for n = 2.
The factorial algorithm multiplies integers from n down to 1.
The factorial algorithm has linear time complexity, so memorization is less beneficial.
Demonstrate the recursive factorial algorithm with test cases.
Conclude the tutorial on recursion and memorization.
